task: I got a notice from a landlord that doesn’t look right, and I’m not sure how hard to push back without making it worse. Can you help me draft a short, firm reply and tell me what I should keep in writing?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a short, firm reply you can use.

## Reply
> I received your notice. I do not agree with the issue as written. Please send me the basis for this notice in writing, including the date, amount, and any photos, records, or lease section you are relying on. I am keeping a copy of this message for my records.

## If you want it a little firmer
> I received your notice and dispute it as written. Please provide the basis for the notice in writing, including the date, amount, photos, records, and the lease or rule section you are relying on. Until then, I do not agree to the charge or claim.

## What to keep in writing
Save:
- the notice itself
- your reply
- any texts, emails, or portal messages
- photos of the issue
- lease pages
- receipts
- dates and times of any calls
- names of who you spoke with
- any follow-up they send

## Good rule
- **Don’t argue by phone only**
- **Don’t admit fault if you’re disputing it**
- **Ask for the exact basis in writing**
- **Keep everything dated**

If you want, I can make this into a **very blunt tenant reply** depending on whether the notice is about rent, damage, entry, or eviction.

user_simulator.text:
Good enough for a first pass. But I’d want it tighter. Less talk, more teeth.

Make it say: I dispute this. Put the exact reason in writing. Keep all contact in writing from here on. No phone calls.

And tell me what to save besides the notice and replies — like if they slip in some crap on the portal, or send a voicemail, does that count too? I’m not playing games with a landlord.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s the tighter version.

## Reply
> I dispute this notice. Put the exact reason in writing. Keep all contact in writing from here on. No phone calls.

## Save everything
Keep:
- the notice
- your reply
- portal messages
- emails
- texts
- voicemails
- letters
- photos
- lease pages
- receipts
- notes from any in-person conversation
- dates and times of every contact

## Yes, these count too
- **Portal messages**: save screenshots or download them
- **Voicemails**: save the audio if you can, or note the date/time and what was said
- **Texts**: screenshot them
- **Emails**: keep the full message, not just the subject line
- **In-person talk**: write down who, when, where, and what they said

## Simple rule
If they said it, sent it, or posted it, save it.

If you want, I can make this into a **one-paragraph landlord reply** that sounds a little more formal or a little more hard-edged.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s it. Short and sharp.

And yes — save all of it. Portal crap counts. Voicemail counts. Anything they put in writing, anything they post, anything you can capture, save it. Screenshot the portal before they change it. Keep the voicemail if you can, or at least write down the date, time, number, and what they said. Don’t let them slide anything past you.

I’d keep a folder on paper and one on the phone if it were me. No phone-only nonsense.
